# io.tsinfer { #tspaint.io.tsinfer }

```python
tspaint.io.tsinfer(source)
```

Infer a tree sequence from genotypes via tsinfer (CLAUDE.md §5, §9).

Produces the realistic substrate where tree accuracy, rather than the true ARG, becomes the
binding constraint (§9).

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**source**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`) or [str](`str`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The genotypes to infer from — a tree sequence carrying variant sites (e.g. from :func:`add_mutations`), a **VCF Zarr** store, or a **VCF** file (see :mod:`tspaint.io_genotypes` for the unified handling and its v1 limits).

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [[tskit](`tskit`).[TreeSequence](`tskit.TreeSequence`)]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   The tsinfer-inferred tree sequence. Sample nodes are preserved in input order, so per-sample labels and truth transfer by sample id.

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

A **zarr** source is read chunked via ``tsinfer.VariantData``
(:func:`tspaint.io_genotypes.variant_data_from_zarr`) — scalable to whole-genome data; a **ts**
source uses ``SampleData.from_tree_sequence`` (deprecated in tsinfer 0.5 but valid while tsinfer
is pinned ``<0.6``); a **VCF** is parsed in-memory by :mod:`tspaint.io_genotypes`.
